batch_metadata.batch_character: Mixed. Predominantly cooperative/administrative (hospital illness coordination Jun 3-4, birthday party planning, summer activities, recurring payment disputes). One significant conflict cluster Jul 14-15 around Olivia's baptism/birthday events. Birthday context creates compound event at batch close.

messages.text: It's been over a week since I told you about how Olivia was feeling and that you need to do better to make sure that she knows you love her
You actually haven't seen her since May 23 except to take her to a doctors appointment when I was sick

primary_flags.analysis: Melissa deploys Olivia's emotional state ('how Olivia was feeling') as a guilt instrument — invoking the child's feelings about Eli's presence without quoting Olivia directly. Paired with a false/hyperbolic attendance claim ('haven't seen her since May 23 except to take her to a doctors appointment when I was sick') and an inadequate-father directive ('need to do better to make sure she knows you love her'). ADD-002 territory (contemptuous parenting judgment). ARC-RF04-001 signal: inadequate-father guilt-induction pattern. Not a full DARVO (no triggering context); the attack arrives as unsolicited parenting critique. Sev 1 per ADD-001 (no full DARVO, no contemptuous phrase, single directive).

messages.text: Can't even eat dinner with your daughter on her bday as she's begging you
It's not appropriate for us to be hanging out like a family.  How come I couldn't take her myself?
First of all you didn't ask to take her on your urn. You've never asked to do anything with her. We made plans cause that's what a good pare[nt]...
You just said you couldn't then walked away
Actually said you couldn't then muttered something that might've been hang on them walked away and got in your car
Third we're not hanging out like a family. We are supporting our daughter and making her happy on her birthday
We all know it's Abby that's not letting you go because she is insecure and immature
You should dump her and date an honest person that will support your relationship with your daughter
Olivia and I are going to dinner. She asked if you could come. I told her I was fine with it
I am not getting into that with you. That is a whole other thing.
There's nothing to get into
You're a shit dad
You muttered something then walked off and went into your car
Then we tried to call you and you wouldn't answer
I said let me put my bag away.
That's not what you said. You got in your car and sat in there
That's not putting your bag away
Your lies don't work with me
I know better

primary_flags.analysis: Birthday event conflict spanning Jul 14-15 produces a compound multi-mechanism attack. Structural sequence: (1) Child guilt lever — ROW-2924 invokes Olivia 'begging' as emotional pressure against Eli's decision not to join a family dinner. Melissa narrates Olivia's emotional state as weapon rather than relaying it neutrally (ARC-RF04-002 signal 6). (2) Inadequate-father false characterization — ROW-2926 'You've never asked to do anything with her' is a sweeping false claim contradicted by the record (Eli coordinated visits, pickups, and activities throughout). Paired with implied good-parent contrast: 'that's what a good parent' frames Eli as the bad one. ARC-RF04-001 signal. (3) Abby-as-obstacle direct causation framing — ROW-2935 'We all know it's Abby that's not letting you go because she is insecure and immature.' Assigns causation of Eli's decision to Abby's character defect. 'We all know' universalizes the claim. (4) Relationship control/interference — ROW-2936 'You should dump her and date an honest person that will support your relationship with your daughter.' Direct instruction to terminate relationship, implying Abby is dishonest and undermines Eli's parenting. (5) Contemptuous character attack — ROW-2940 'You're a shit dad.' Delivered after Eli declines to engage further: contempt delivered as apparent final word. (6) Gaslighting tail — ROW-2947-2951: Eli states 'I said let me put my bag away'; Melissa responds 'That's not what you said. You got in your car and sat in there' / 'Your lies don't work with me / I know better.' Direct assertion that Eli is lying about his own words and actions; claim of superior epistemic access. Sev 2: multi-attack compound event with child leverage, contemptuous character attack, relationship interference, and gaslighting sequence active simultaneously. COMP-RF04-008.

peripheral_flags.text: No calling and checking on her?
peripheral_flags.note: Mild guilt induction following Eli's 'No update?' while Olivia was hospitalized. Hospital context reduces severity — some justification for frustration. Peripheral only.

peripheral_flags.text: All day? / And you didn't try again? / Ummm you're not the boss of me
peripheral_flags.note: Contemptuous dismissal cluster triggered by Eli's relaying of availability message. 'All day?' and 'And you didn't try again?' are contemptuous challenges; 'you're not the boss of me' is defiant refusal of co-parenting coordination. Mild contempt cluster — below primary threshold but pattern-consistent.

peripheral_flags.text: Don't be ruining my night either
peripheral_flags.note: Unprovoked framing of Eli's status check ('You guys still there?') as a threat to 'ruin' Melissa's night. Passive-aggressive contempt. Peripheral.

Eli asks if Olivia wants to be picked up tomorrow; Melissa relays Olivia's response as 'I guess.' Editorial relay — lukewarm child response conveyed in a way designed to make Eli feel unwanted. ADD-003 territory. Borderline ARC-RF04-002 candidate; kept peripheral given brevity and ambiguity.

peripheral_flags.text: Speaking of you working and making money you are way behind on paying the daycare and swim / When you plan on paying that
peripheral_flags.note: Melissa pivots from swim-lesson scheduling to payment demand with 'speaking of you working and making money' framing — implicitly attacking Eli's character/priorities by connecting employment to non-payment. Peripheral RF-04/RF-05 overlap.

peripheral_flags.text: I'm going to hit you with a contempt of you don't pay
peripheral_flags.note: Legal threat in context of payment dispute. Primary RF-05 territory; RF-04 peripheral (coercive pressure). Kept peripheral given legitimate financial dispute context.

ARC-RF04-001.signal_summary: S22: ROW-2719-2720 (Jun 15) — inadequate-father guilt induction using Olivia's feelings as instrument. S23: ROW-2924-2951 (Jul 14-15) — birthday compound anchor signals: 'you've never asked to do anything with her' false-characterization + 'you're a shit dad' contemptuous attack.

ARC-RF04-002.signal_summary: S06: ROW-2924 (Jul 14) — 'as she's begging you' birthday guilt lever. Melissa narrates Olivia's emotional state to pressure Eli into joining family birthday dinner. Mechanism: child's emotional state invoked as manipulative instrument, not neutral relay.

compound_pattern_detected.label: Birthday Event Compound — Child Guilt + Abby Attack + Contempt + Gaslighting
compound_pattern_detected.description: RF-04 (primary, Sev 2 — child-mediated guilt 'begging you,' false-inadequate-father 'never asked to do anything,' contemptuous attack 'you're a shit dad') + RF-01 (ROW-2935-2936: Abby-as-obstacle direct causation 'it's Abby that's not letting you go,' inadequate-father framing 'never asked to do anything') + RF-05 (ROW-2936: relationship interference 'you should dump her,' implied coercive pressure) + RF-09 (ROW-2948-2951: gaslighting sequence — Eli's statement about his own actions contradicted as lies, 'your lies don't work with me / I know better').

cp3_items_generated: CP3-RF04-008: ROW-2924-2951 (Jul 14-15 2023) birthday event compound — Abby 'insecure and immature' direct attribution + 'dump her' relationship interference instruction + 'you're a shit dad' contempt + gaslighting 'your lies don't work with me / I know better.' COMP-RF04-008 (RF-04+RF-01+RF-05+RF-09). Note Abby-as-obstacle escalation context: prior B-007 peak was 'bitch can shut her fucking whore mouth'; this batch deploys covert attribution framing ('we all know it's Abby') + paternalistic control ('dump her') in a birthday/child-presence context.

notable_observations.cooperative_zones: Jun 3-13: hospital/illness coordination, birthday party planning, swim/activities — largely cooperative with no RF-04 signals despite extended engagement. Medical coordination period shows Melissa and Eli functioning without escalation.
notable_observations.abby_framework_update: ROW-2935 introduces a new Abby-as-obstacle framing variant: causal attribution ('it's Abby that's not letting you'). Previous deployments attacked Abby directly or framed her as an obstacle to Olivia. This deployment blames Abby for Eli's autonomous decision-making, framing him as under Abby's control. Paired with ROW-2936's 'dump her' — seeks to end relationship by characterizing Abby as a controlling actor who harms Eli's parenting.
notable_observations.birthday_context_note: The Jul 14-15 conflict is anchored in Olivia's birthday events (baptism Jul 13, birthday party Jul 14-15). The child-occasion context amplifies the emotional manipulation: Melissa weaponizes the child's birthday as a frame that makes any deviation from her preferred arrangement appear as abandonment. 'Can't even eat dinner with your daughter on her bday as she's begging you' embeds the child's birthday as moral weight.
notable_observations.gaslighting_density: ROW-2948-2951 four-message sequence is a concentrated gaslighting deployment — asserting authoritative knowledge of what Eli said over Eli's own account ('That's not what you said' / 'Your lies don't work with me / I know better'). Primary RF-09 candidates for Phase 2 synthesis.
